articles.title: BU rally pushes back on Pride flag removals, calls for policy change
articles.description:
Organizers handed a letter to university officials, asking for a revision of the signage policy.

articles.title: She knit an MBTA-themed sweater inspired by her commutes. The internet is loving it.
articles.description:

articles.title: Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill
articles.description:
After years of cheering runners in Wellesley, 18-year-old Emmie Laudano will run Boston to support the Tierney Learning Center.

articles.title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
articles.description:

articles.title: Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss
articles.description:

articles.title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
articles.description:
The man imported the parts from dealers in Cameroon and Indonesia, who would hunt and kill the animals he requested.

articles.title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
articles.description:

articles.title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
articles.description:
The lieutenant allegedly told the Middlesex DA’s security director to take information about the sergeant’s crash “to the grave.”

articles.title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
articles.description:
The foster child died in the couple’s custody in 2020 after they ignored “clear and obvious signs” that he needed medical treatment.

articles.title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
articles.description:
Judy Church, 67, was sentenced to life in prison without parole for fatally poisoning her boyfriend's Powerade drink with antifreeze.

The post Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend appeared first on Boston.com.
